records.ety: [OE. impugnen, F. impugner, fr. L. impugnare; in on, against + pugnare to flight. See Pugnacious.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Impugned (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Impugning.]

defs: To attack by words or arguments; to contradict; to assail; to call in question; to make insinuations against; to gainsay; to oppose; as, to impugn a person's integrity.
quotes: The truth hereof I will not rashly impugn, or overboldly affirm.
